evidence: Public page listed AI and automation categories including AI integration developers, chatbot developers, AI developers, automation experts, N8N experts, and AI consultants.

Public page displayed sample AI jobs including AI Agent Engineer, AI Product Builder, work support for a senior gen AI role, AI Coaching Platform Developer, AI Social Media Operator, and AI Automation Specialist.
Public page displayed an AI engineer median hourly rate range of USD 35-60.
Upwork reported AI-enabled skills grew 109% year over year in its 2026 in-demand skills release.
The release highlighted AI integration, AI data annotation and labeling, AI chatbot development, and AI video generation and editing as growth areas.
The report also listed stable adjacent categories such as full-stack development, data analytics, scripting and automation, lead generation, and marketing automation.
Existing local AI workflow audit offer packet exists.
Existing synthetic agency reporting proof asset exists.
Existing Upwork profile/proposal draft exists and is marked local-only.

archetypes.public_signal: AI Agent Engineer for automated social media content and publishing workflow.
archetypes.buyer_pain: The buyer wants agentic content operations, but needs supervision, approvals, and predictable handoff points.
forbidden_claims: No promise of fully autonomous posting

archetypes.public_signal: AI Product Builder / CRM project sample and AI-enabled product work.
archetypes.buyer_pain: The buyer wants a product/CRM concept shaped into implementable workflow and data boundaries.
forbidden_claims: No promise of production CRM delivery without scoped contract
No handling credentials or client systems
No fixed revenue or conversion claim

archetypes.public_signal: Gen AI role sample referenced agentic workflows and LangChain/LangGraph-style architecture.
archetypes.buyer_pain: The buyer needs architecture support for agents and workflow orchestration, often before execution code is safe.
forbidden_claims: No claim of deploying live agents without approval

archetypes.public_signal: AI Coaching Platform Developer sample and chatbot development demand signal.
archetypes.buyer_pain: The buyer wants an AI assistant or coaching/support app with human-safe boundaries.
archetypes.offer_name: AI Assistant Safety And Scope Blueprint
local_asset_fit: agent skill starter kit local product
service catalog and gate checklist artifacts
forbidden_claims: No compliance or safety certification claim
No use of real user data without approval

archetypes.public_signal: AI Automation Specialist sample referenced workflows and integrations such as Sheets, Gmail, Slack, and CRMs.
archetypes.buyer_pain: The buyer wants practical automation across familiar tools without breaking privacy, access, or approvals.
forbidden_claims: No access to Gmail/Slack/CRM without contract and approval
No guaranteed time savings for real clients

recommended_first_offer.why: It uses the strongest local proof already built and aligns with public AI-agent/automation demand without needing credentials or platform action.
draft_scope: Map one described workflow from client-provided plain-language notes.
Identify safe automation candidates and human approval points.
Produce one synthetic proof packet with no private client data.
Deliver a next-step blueprint rather than a production build.
not_in_scope: Credentials, private data, or public posting.

acceptance_checks: Five archetypes are sourced from public job text or category summaries.
Every offer avoids claims of guaranteed client results.
No Upwork account, proposal, message, or profile action is performed.
Each archetype maps to at least one local evidence asset.
next_action: Create a no-send Upwork approval request packet for the recommended first offer only after human review; keep account, proposal, payment, client data, credentials, public action, worker/runtime, model/MCP, and external side effects blocked.
